title: Rocket Lab wins contract for three more iQPS launches
summary: Rocket Lab has secured a contract with Japanese radar satellite company iQPS for three additional Electron launches. This agreement expands their ongoing partnership in deploying radar satellites.

title: Falcon 9 launches Cygnus cargo spacecraft to the ISS
summary: A SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket launched a Northrop Grumman Cygnus cargo spacecraft to the International Space Station on April 11. This mission highlights Northrop Grumman's reliance on a competitor's rocket for ISS resupply.

title: Moog Technology Keeps Artemis II Astronauts Safe During Historic Lunar Mission
summary: Moog Inc. provided critical technology to ensure the safety of Artemis II astronauts during their historic lunar mission. The company's precision motion and fluid control systems played a key role in the mission.

title: Orion splashes down to successfully end Artemis 2 mission
summary: The Artemis 2 mission, the first human mission beyond Earth orbit in over 50 years, successfully concluded with the Orion spacecraft splashing down in the Pacific Ocean on April 10.

title: HawkEye 360 files to go public
summary: HawkEye 360, a space-based radio-frequency signals intelligence company, has filed to go public. The company, based in Herndon, Virginia, serves government agencies with its specialized services.

title: Spacety raises $190 million to scale satellite manufacturing, plans IPO
summary: Chinese satellite manufacturer Spacety secured $190 million in funding to expand its vertically integrated satellite production and data services. The company also announced plans for an IPO to further support its growth.

title: GSOA and Novaspace Launch the Space Industry Forum (SIF) 2026 in Singapore
summary: GSOA and Novaspace are launching the Space Industry Forum (SIF) 2026, a new flagship event scheduled for May 19, 2026, at The Fullerton Hotel in Singapore.

title: Commerce Department budget proposal revives concerns about TraCSS
summary: A budget proposal for the Department of Commerce has raised concerns about the future of the civil space traffic management system (TraCSS) under development. This could impact the ongoing efforts to manage space traffic and debris.

title: China’s Chang’e-7 arrives at spaceport for lunar south pole exploration mission
summary: China's Chang'e-7 lunar spacecraft has arrived at Wenchang spaceport for launch preparations. The mission is planned for liftoff in the second half of 2026 to explore the lunar south pole.

title: NASA prepares for Artemis 2 return
summary: NASA is preparing for the Artemis 2 mission's return, with the spacecraft scheduled to reenter on April 10 before splashing down off the California coast.

title: From spa to boat party: 10 epic events for the Aug. 12, 2026, total solar eclipse in Spain and Iceland
summary: The news highlights various events planned for the total solar eclipse on August 12, 2026, in Spain and Iceland, including festivals, spa sessions, and skywatching activities. This event is positioned as a major attraction for eclipse enthusiasts.

title: 'The most special thing that will ever happen in my life': Artemis 2 astronauts describe their epic moon mission
summary: The Artemis 2 astronauts have returned to Earth after their historic moon mission and are still processing the experience. They describe the mission as the most special thing in their lives but struggle to articulate its significance.

title: This Week In Space podcast: Episode 205 — All About Artemis
summary: The latest episode of the 'This Week In Space' podcast covers details about the Artemis 2 mission. Rod Pyle and Tariq Malik discuss the flight of Artemis 2.

title: SpaceX launches huge 'Cygnus XL' cargo ship carrying over 5 tons of supplies to ISS astronauts (video)
summary: SpaceX launched a large 'Cygnus XL' cargo ship carrying over 5 tons of supplies to ISS astronauts. This is Northrop Grumman's second 'Cygnus XL' mission to the International Space Station.

title: Artemis 3 and beyond: What's next for NASA after Artemis 2 moon success
summary: NASA is not resting after the successful Artemis 2 moon mission and has ambitious plans for future lunar exploration, including Artemis 3 and beyond. The agency aims to continue advancing its lunar program following this milestone.

title: Splashdown! Artemis 2 astronauts return to Earth after historic NASA mission to the moon
summary: NASA's Artemis 2 astronauts successfully returned to Earth on April 10, completing a historic mission around the moon. The mission broke spaceflight records and paved the way for future lunar missions.

title: NEAF 2026 returns to New York this weekend with all-star astronaut lineup and 'world's largest' space expo
summary: NEAF 2026, the world's largest space expo, returns to New York with an all-star astronaut lineup. The event will gather thousands of space enthusiasts, professionals, and skywatchers.

title: The Artemis 2 astronauts saw a rare solar eclipse from beyond the moon. Here's what it looked like
summary: The Artemis 2 astronauts witnessed a rare solar eclipse from beyond the moon, captured in a NASA timelapse showing the moon crossing the sun and revealing the solar corona. This provided a unique extended view of totality from deep space.

title: Why I'm hunting for Comet Pan-STARRS right now — before it's too late
summary: A rare long-period comet, C/2025 R3 (Pan-STARRS), is visible before sunrise in April after 170,000 years, with key viewing dates and tips provided.

title: 'They’re not shipping cows up to Mars': 'For All Mankind' creators talk scientific accuracy and colonial inspirations behind season 5 (interview)
summary: The creators of the TV show 'For All Mankind' discuss the scientific accuracy and colonial inspirations behind season 5, emphasizing that the series does not feature unrealistic elements like shipping cows to Mars.

title: La NASA da la bienvenida a la Tierra a los exploradores lunares de Artemis II, quienes batieron récords
summary: Los astronautas de la misión Artemis II de la NASA, incluyendo a Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch y Jeremy Hansen, regresaron a la Tierra tras batir récords en su vuelo de prueba a la Luna, el primero en más de 50 años. La misión marca un hito clave para el programa Artemis de la NASA.

title: NASA Science, Cargo Launch Aboard Northrop Grumman CRS-24
summary: NASA launched the Northrop Grumman CRS-24 mission to the International Space Station, carrying 11,000 pounds of cargo including science and technology payloads. The Cygnus XL spacecraft lifted off successfully at 7:41 a.m. EDT on Saturday.

title: Artemis II Splashes Down
summary: NASA's Orion spacecraft splashed down in the Pacific Ocean on April 10, 2026, after the Artemis II mission achieved milestones including the farthest crewed spaceflight record and views of the Moon's far side.

title: NASA Welcomes Record-Setting Artemis II Moonfarers Back to Earth
summary: NASA's Artemis II mission, carrying astronauts Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch, and CSA's Jeremy Hansen, successfully splashed down after a historic Moon flyby, marking the first crewed lunar mission in over 50 years.

title: New Perspective of Home
summary: Artemis II's lunar flyby on April 6, 2026, will capture a unique perspective of Earth and the Moon aligned in the same frame. The Moon's surface will appear in sharp detail, with Earth visible in the background, softly lit by the Sun.

title: Earthset From the Lunar Far Side
summary: NASA's Artemis II mission crew captured stunning images of Earth from the far side of the Moon during their lunar journey. This milestone highlights the mission's progress toward future crewed lunar exploration.

title: Indoor Testing Facilities available at the NASA Unmanned Autonomy Research Complex (NUARC)
summary: NASA's Unmanned Autonomy Research Complex (NUARC) offers indoor testing facilities like the WindShaper fan array for dynamic low-speed and hovering flight research, capable of generating arbitrary wind gradients via a Python API. A companion WindProbe uses OptiTrack motion capture for flow surveys.

title: I Am Artemis: Dan Florez
summary: Dan Florez, test director for NASA’s Exploration Ground Systems Program, recounts witnessing the Artemis I launch from Kennedy Space Center's Launch Control Center on November 16, 2022. The event marked a historic moment as NASA's Artemis I mission engines ignited at 1:47 a.m. EST.

title: Indoor Testing Facilities available at the NASA Unmanned Autonomy Research Complex (NUARC)
summary: NASA's Unmanned Autonomy Research Complex (NUARC) now offers indoor testing facilities with a WindShaper fan array for dynamic low-speed and hovering flight research, including arbitrary wind gradient generation via Python API. A companion WindProbe is also available for flow surveys using the lab's OptiTrack motion capture system.

title: Starstruck
summary: The Artemis II crew captured a photo of the Milky Way on April 7, 2026, showcasing its spiral structure. The galaxy spans over 100,000 light-years, with Earth located along one of its spiral arms.

title: Ship 39 and Booster 19 Roll out for Static Fire Testing
summary: Ship 39 and Booster 19 have been rolled out for static fire testing as part of the Artemis program. This follows the successful return of Orion.

title: Falcon 9 launches CRS NG-24 cargo ship to ISS
summary: SpaceX's Falcon 9 successfully launched the CRS NG-24 cargo mission to the International Space Station (ISS). This follows Northrop Grumman's recent CRS NG-23 mission completion.

title: Artemis II returns to Earth after historic 10-day mission around the Moon
summary: Artemis II completed a historic 10-day mission around the Moon, traveling over 800,000 km before returning to Earth. This marks a significant milestone in NASA's lunar exploration efforts.

title: Artemis II closing in on Earth return
summary: Artemis II is nearing its return to Earth after a historic 10-day mission that took humans farther from Earth than ever before. The mission marks a significant milestone in space exploration.

title: China debuts new launchers, tests orbital servicing and outlines future deep-space missions
summary: China has introduced new commercial launchers from CAS Space and Space Pioneer, while also testing orbital servicing capabilities and outlining future deep-space missions.

title: SpaceX continues refinements at Starbase Pad 2 ahead of Booster 19 return
summary: SpaceX is making final preparations at Starbase Pad 2 for the return of Booster 19. The refinements are part of ongoing efforts to support future launch operations.

title: Artemis II breaks record, conducts lunar flyby
summary: Artemis II achieved a record-breaking lunar flyby with the Orion spacecraft, carrying astronauts Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch, and Jeremy Hansen. This mission marks a significant milestone in NASA's Artemis program.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9 to loft Cygnus to ISS, Minotaur IV to launch from California
summary: A Falcon 9 rocket is set to launch a Cygnus spacecraft to the International Space Station (ISS), while a Minotaur IV rocket is scheduled to launch from California. This comes as NASA's Artemis II mission reaches its halfway point.

title: Orion Spacecraft Races Toward Historic Lunar Flyby in Artemis II Mission
summary: NASA's Artemis II mission is in its critical mid-flight phase as the Orion spacecraft approaches a historic lunar flyby. This marks the first crewed mission in the Artemis program, carrying astronauts toward the Moon.

title: Artemis II Orion performs TLI Burn — Now headed to the Moon
summary: NASA's Orion spacecraft successfully performed the Trans-Lunar Injection (TLI) burn, marking a historic milestone as it heads to the Moon for the Artemis II mission, the first crewed lunar mission in over 50 years.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) released a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over its 50-year history. This publication commemorates the agency's milestones and contributions to space exploration.

title: Register for ESA’s Industry Space Days 2026
summary: Registration is now open for ESA’s Industry Space Days 2026, an event scheduled for 16–17 September at the agency’s technical centre in Noordwijk, The Netherlands.

title: Pointing in the (b)right direction
summary: The article discusses advancements in spacecraft pointing technology, highlighting improvements in precision and efficiency. It emphasizes the significance of these innovations for future space missions.

title: Graphene and lasers for space propulsion
summary: An international research team demonstrated light-based propulsion using graphene aerogels in microgravity during ESA's parabolic flight campaign. The study, published in Advanced Science, showed that lasers can propel these ultralight materials with greater efficiency in space-like conditions.

title: Graphene and lasers for space propulsion
summary: Graphene and lasers could revolutionize space propulsion by steering solar sails and adjusting satellite positions. An experiment on a gravity rollercoaster demonstrated this potential.

title: A silicon chip that can do it all
summary: A breakthrough silicon chip has been developed that integrates multiple functions traditionally requiring separate components. This innovation could revolutionize space electronics by reducing size, weight, and power consumption for spacecraft systems.

title: Proba-3’s Coronagraph is alive!
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft has re-established contact with ground control after a month-long anomaly. The mission team reports that the spacecraft is now operational and communicating.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: ESA is hosting events to present the follow-up to the CM25 meeting, detailing planned activities and industrial opportunities across ESA programmes.

title: Plato readies for space-like tests
summary: The European Space Agency's Plato mission, designed to discover Earth-like exoplanets, is undergoing critical space-like tests in ESA's Large Space Simulator. The spacecraft, equipped with 26 ultrasensitive cameras, will be launched on an Ariane 6 rocket by Arianespace in January 2027.

title: Albedo Ratchets Up the Power for its Second VLEO Flight
summary: Albedo unveiled a new VLEO-capable satellite bus designed for its second orbital mission, scheduled for 2027. This marks a significant step in advancing the company's capabilities in Very Low Earth Orbit (VLEO) satellite technology.

title: Moog’s “Tip to Tail” Contributions to the Artemis II Flight
summary: Moog is supplying over 100 components for the Artemis II mission, covering the entire rocket and Orion spacecraft. The company's hardware spans from the tip to the tail of the vehicle.

title: Artemis II Gives Airbus Hope For European Spaceflight
summary: Artemis II features the European Service Module (ESM), built by Airbus Defence and Space, marking a significant step for European spaceflight involvement. This collaboration between Airbus and ESA highlights Europe's growing role in NASA's lunar missions.

title: Portal Closes $50M Series A
summary: Portal has secured a $50 million Series A funding round led by Geodesic Capital and Mach33, with participation from Booz Allen Ventures, Ark Invest, AlleyCorp, and FUSE.

title: The State of Launch 2026
summary: Rocket Lab's VP of launch discusses how constraints like the Golden Dome program can influence competition in the space industry by requiring companies to meet deadlines to maintain eligibility. Failure to meet these deadlines could result in elimination from competition.

title: Vantor Unveils New Sat Classes: Vantage and Pulse
summary: Vantor introduced two new satellite classes, Vantage and Pulse, aimed at enhancing imagery resolution and revisit rate. This development marks a significant step in the company's satellite technology advancements.

title: The State of ISAM 2026
summary: The article discusses the growing complexity of In-Space Servicing, Assembly, and Manufacturing (ISAM) by 2026, noting that it can no longer be treated as a single concept. It highlights the expanding scope and challenges of ISAM as a key trend in the space industry.

title: Czech Radiation Chips on Artemis II Bring ISS Experience
summary: Czech-made radiation chips will be part of the Artemis II mission, leveraging ISS experience to monitor solar flares during solar maximum. This technology serves as an early warning system for potential solar events.

title: Advocates Ready For NASA Science Funding Fight, Part II
summary: NASA's proposed $18.8 billion budget faces opposition from advocates and lawmakers due to significant cuts to science and STEM missions. They vow to resist the reductions as they did in the past.

title: Starfish Closes $100M+ Series B
summary: Starfish Space has raised over $100 million in a Series B funding round led by Point72 Ventures, with co-leads Activate Capital and Shield Capital. This investment will support the company's growth in the space industry.

title: Emirates Connectivity Exec Explains the Airline’s Move to Starlink
summary: Emirates announced its adoption of Starlink for in-flight Wi-Fi across its entire fleet, marking a significant shift in the airline's connectivity services. The move highlights the growing integration of satellite-based internet solutions in commercial aviation.

title: Amazon CEO Targets Mid-2026 Launch for Amazon Leo Service
summary: Amazon CEO Andy Jassy announced that Amazon's satellite constellation service, Amazon Leo, is scheduled to launch in mid-2026. The exact launch date was not specified in the letter to shareholders.

title: BryceTech Report Shows SpaceX Accounted for 50% of Launches in 2025
summary: BryceTech's 2025 Year in Review reveals SpaceX conducted half of all global launches in 2025. The report highlights a growing trend toward small satellite deployments.

title: EnduroSat and Shield Space Strike European Partnership for Defense Missions
summary: EnduroSat and Shield Space have formed a strategic partnership to jointly operate space defense missions in Europe. The collaboration aims to leverage their combined technologies for on-orbit operations.

title: Network Integration Remains a Challenge in Pushing Data to the Tactical Edge
summary: Commercial space leaders are working to push data from centralized nodes to the tactical edge for faster decision-making in conflicts. Network integration remains a key challenge in this effort.

title: Vantor Introduces New Satellite Classes for High Resolution Imaging, Frequent Revisits
summary: Vantor launched two new classes of electro-optical imaging satellites designed for high-resolution imagery and frequent revisits. This expansion aims to enhance commercial satellite operations and spatial intelligence services.

title: Space Force Awards 14 Companies Space Domain Awareness Contracts Under “Andromeda”
summary: The U.S. Space Force awarded 14 companies over $1.8 billion in contracts for Space Domain Awareness under the 'Andromeda' program. The contracts, managed by Space Systems Command, focus on Geosynchronous Reconnaissance.

title: NSG Names Haithem Alfaraj as its Second CEO
summary: Neo Space Group (NSG), a Saudi space company, appointed Haithem Mohammed Mojil Alfaraj as its new CEO, replacing Martijn Blanken who led the company since its inception.

title: In-Space Maneuverability Startup Portal Raises Series A as it Shifts into Production Mode
summary: Portal Space Systems raised $50 million in a Series A funding round to advance the development of maneuverable spacecraft and expand its manufacturing capacity. The startup is transitioning into production mode as it grows its team.

title: Live in the Booth: Sky Perfect JSAT President & CEO Eiichi Yonekura
summary: Sky Perfect JSAT, a major global satellite operator, is focusing on a multi-faceted strategy to expand its business in the coming years. Eiichi Yonekura, the company's President & CEO, discussed these plans in a live interview.
